findings.issue: Regex::new(...).unwrap() in production benchmark code path. The pattern r"(?s)func\s+main\s*\(\s*\)\s*\{[^}]*\}" is called at runtime inside the Go test harness assembly function. If the pattern were ever corrupted or changed to an invalid regex, this would panic the benchmark runner process with no recovery.
findings.recommendation: Replace with Regex::new(...).map_err(|e| AvaError::ToolError(format!("invalid regex: {e}")))? or hoist to a LazyLock<Regex> static with a regexes_compile() test.

findings.issue: Multiple std::fs::read_to_string() calls in synchronous methods (snapshot_before_edit, record_edit_complete, stream_chunk, flush_incomplete) that are called from async contexts in the agent loop. The blocking sync variants exist alongside async versions (snapshot_before_edit_async), but callers in tool_execution.rs may invoke the sync variant from an async task context.
findings.recommendation: Audit all call sites to confirm sync variants are only called from spawn_blocking; deprecate or remove the sync variants if async versions cover all call sites.

findings.issue: append_trace_event() uses std::fs::create_dir_all and std::fs::OpenOptions (blocking I/O) without spawn_blocking. The function is called from async code in the agent loop after every tool call. Blocking the executor thread on file I/O degrades throughput under load.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the I/O block in tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || { ... }); the error-swallowing pattern can remain since tracing failures should not abort the agent.

findings.issue: TODO comment flags that test/lint command composition still builds shell strings passed to `sh -c` instead of using direct argv builders. Although shell_single_quote() mitigates injection, the comment explicitly notes the remaining risk. The same issue exists symmetrically in test_runner.rs (line 56).
findings.recommendation: Replace sh -c string construction with std::process::Command argument vectors (argv builders) for auto-detected commands. Custom commands (user-supplied) are already validated via classify_bash_command, so the risk is primarily in the detect_lint_command / detect_test_command auto-detection paths.

findings.issue: TODO: code_search tool rebuilds a full CodebaseIndex on every invocation. In a project with many files this is O(n) disk I/O per tool call, creating latency spikes during multi-tool agent turns. The comment acknowledges this is a performance issue.
findings.recommendation: Store a shared Arc<RwLock<CodebaseIndex>> on AgentStack (alongside the existing codebase_index field) and pass it into the tool's execute() context, reusing the already-built index from stack initialization.

findings.issue: eprintln! used for progress output in the `ava review` CLI command (lines 39, 42-46, 74). While this is standard for CLI tools, it bypasses the tracing infrastructure so these messages do not appear in structured log files and cannot be filtered or redirected via -v flags.
findings.recommendation: Replace eprintln! with tracing::info! so review progress integrates with the -v/-vv/-vvv verbosity system and structured log output.

findings.issue: Fire-and-forget tokio::spawn for set_thinking_level, cycle_thinking, and set_mode log errors via warn! but do not surface them to the TUI event loop. If AgentStack::set_thinking_level() fails (e.g., due to a poisoned RwLock), the TUI will silently display the wrong thinking level. This was flagged in round-3 focus list as still-open.
findings.recommendation: Return a JoinHandle from each helper, poll it with a timeout in the next event tick, and emit an AppEvent::Error on failure. Alternatively, make these methods synchronous via try_write() and return an immediate error that can be surfaced as a toast notification.

findings.issue: TODO comment notes missing per-session sequence column for stable message ordering. The module is marked Legacy and not actively used, but if it is ever queried it relies on created_at string ordering which breaks across timezone or clock-skew boundaries.
findings.recommendation: Either add the sequence column in a migration or add a clear #[deprecated] attribute to the entire module pointing to ava-session as the canonical store.

findings.issue: TODO in doc comment: plugin auth hook point for provider credential injection is not wired into AgentStack or the provider creation call site. This means plugins cannot provide OAuth credentials at provider creation time.
findings.recommendation: Track this as a backlog item rather than a doc-comment TODO; move the TODO to docs/development/backlog.md and remove the comment from production source.

findings.issue: TODO: validate_targets() does not validate routing targets against the model registry at config load time because the registry initializes after config. The method logs a debug message but performs no validation.
findings.recommendation: Add a post-init validation call site in AgentStack::new() after the model registry is available, invoking routing_config.validate_targets() and logging warnings for unknown provider/model pairs.
